wrapper_chain: CustomBedrockClient (context limit retry + extended context retry)
diagnosis: RETRY AMPLIFICATION: 4 retry layers can produce up to 108 total attempts for a single user request.

THREAD POOL RISK: Each asyncio.to_thread() call consumes a thread from the default pool (size=14). BedrockProvider uses to_thread for BOTH the initial API call AND every stream chunk read. With retries, a single request can consume 2+ threads for extended periods.

BLOCKING IN CustomBedrockClient: The _retry_with_extended_context method calls self.original_invoke() synchronously inside a to_thread wrapper. If extended context activation fails with a timeout, the thread is blocked for the full boto3 read_timeout (300s) while holding a pool slot.

DEADLOCK VECTOR: With N concurrent requests each consuming 2 threads (connect + stream read), the pool exhausts at N >= pool_size/2. New to_thread calls then block waiting for a free thread, which can't free because the event loop is blocked waiting for to_thread to return.

tests.name: overfill_pool_18_tasks
tests.note: 18 tasks x 2s block, pool_size=14. Expected ~4s for 2 batches.

tests.name: nested_threads_14_tasks
tests.note: Simulates provider->client->boto3 nesting with 14 concurrent requests
